data.title: Goodland Group Trims Attributable Loss in Fiscal H1
data.description:
Goodland Group trimmed its attributable loss to owners during fiscal H1 by 32% to SG$2.3 million from SG$3.4 million a year earlier, according to a Thursday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

Loss per share trimmed to SG$0.0065 compared with SG$0.0095 in the comparable period.

Revenue was up 6% year over year to SG$5.5 million from SG$5.2 million, due to the sale of a development property.

data.title: Japan's Daikin commences mass production of residential air conditioners in Indonesia
data.description:
The Indonesia unit of Japanese air conditioner maker Daikin Industries Ltd said on Friday:

  • COMMENCES MASS PRODUCTION OF RESIDENTIAL AIR CONDITIONER IN ITS INDONESIA FACTORY, EXPECTS TO LAUNCH LOCAL MANUFACTURED AC UNITS BY JULY 2025

data.title: Japanese Shares Flat Amid Weak GDP Data
data.description:
The Nikkei 225 Index ended virtually flat at 37,754 while the broader Topix Index inched up 0.05% to 2,741 on Friday, with Japanese shares struggling for traction as weak economic data weighed on sentiment.

Investor caution prevailed after Japan’s economy contracted 0.2% quarter-on-quarter in Q1, marking the first decline in a year and falling short of expectations for a 0.1% drop.

The data reinforced concerns raised by the Bank of Japan earlier this week, which warned of potential economic moderation due to US trade policy pressures.

Japanese markets also mirrored losses in US technology stocks, which were hit by profit-taking overnight.

Domestic tech names were broadly weaker, with Disco and Advantest down 1.9%, and Tokyo Electron falling 2.8%.

However, select non-tech stocks provided some support.

Mitsubishi UFJ rose 0.9%, Mitsubishi Heavy gained 3.1%, and Sanrio surged 7.4%.

data.title: Allianz Shares Track Progress — Market Talk
data.description: Allianz has made steady progress recently which is already reflected in its share price, HSBC says in a research note. The German insurer's stock is trading above its one- and three-year averages and at a 3% premium to the sector. "We view Allianz's business model favorably with its strong balance sheet, diversification by business line and geography, as well as its focus on organic growth, productivity improvements, and improving economic value creation under its 2024-27 business plan," analysts write. However, they cut the rating on the stock to hold from buy as property and casualty headwinds are moderating and consensus already sits at the top end of management's guided range. Shares have risen by about a third over the year to date. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

data.title: CBOT soybeans may fall toward $10.38-1/2
data.description:
Key points:
  • For more technical analyses, please clickTECH/C

The CBOT soybean July contract (SN25) may fall toward $10.38-1/2 per bushel, as it has broken support at $10.55.

The next support will be at $10.38-1/2. Wave pattern suggests a completion of a wave C from $9.85-1/4, which failed to travel far above its 61.8% projection level of $10.71-1/2.

Separately, the pattern from $9.66 looks like a huge bearish wedge, which formed after a downtrend. The lower trendline points at a target of $10.05-1/2.

On the hourly chart, a retracement analysis on the uptrend from $10.36-3/4 reveals a series of fine-tuned support and resistance levels.

The contract is expected to retest support at $10.47-3/4, a break below which could open the way toward $10.36-3/4.

* Wang Tao is a Reuters market analyst for commodities and energy technicals. Views expressed are his own.

** No information in this analysis should be considered as being business, financial or legal advice. Each reader should consult their own professional or other advisers for business, financial or legal advice regarding the products mentioned in the analyses.

data.title: Goldman Sachs initiates Havas with 'buy' on resilient growth, strong balance sheet
data.description:
** Goldman Sachs initiates coverage on French advertising group Havas (HAV.PA) with a 'buy' rating and a EUR 1.9 target price

** Broker sees three reasons to buy Havas: resilient growth, margin improvement potential, and a strong balance sheet enabling M&A

** GS says Havas is expected to outpace peers in growth for 2025 and maintain similar growth thereafter

** Margin catch-up is anticipated due to restructuring, operational simplification, and a shift to higher-margin businesses, Goldman Sachs adds

** Shares trade at 6.5x P/E and a 13% free cash flow yield, representing a significant discount to peers (30%) and the EU market (54%), broker points out

** Out of 9 analysts that cover Havas NV, six rate the stock "strong buy" or "buy, "​three rate "hold" and no analysts rate the stock "strong sell" or "sell"

data.title: Taiwan's GlobalWafers Plans Additional $4 Billion Investment in U.S.
data.description:
By Kimberley Kao

Taiwanese chip materials maker GlobalWafers said it plans to invest an additional $4 billion in the U.S. as it seeks to strengthen local supply, in line with the Trump administration's efforts to boost manufacturing.

The chip materials maker plans to further grow its U.S. manufacturing base and is more than doubling its existing investment to reach $7.5 billion, Chairperson Doris Hsu announced during the opening ceremony for its first factory in Sherman, Texas, on Thursday.

The company, which is the world's third largest silicon wafer supplier, said that the expansion plans are in line with growing market demand, helped by the "advantageous tariff structure favoring cost effective U.S. expansion."

Plans for the Texas facility, which will make silicon wafers used in semiconductors, were first announced in 2022. The company said the project has created 1,200 construction and 180 permanent jobs in North Texas, and will employ up to 650 engineering, technical and operational professionals by the end of 2028.

The company said the facility is the first production line of its kind built in the U.S. in over two decades, and that the U.S. Department of Commerce is investing $406 million in GlobalWafers' new U.S. operations under the CHIPS Program.

U.S. officials see chip-making as a national-security imperative because economies, technological advancement and military might all are increasingly dependent on who has the best chips. Covid-era supply-chain issues also highlighted the critical nature of the industry when shortages of chips led to slowdowns in auto sales, among other disruptions.

President Trump has repeatedly called for more chip manufacturing in the U.S., flagging concerns of Taiwan's near-monopoly in the sector.

In March, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. announced plans to invest at least $100 billion more in chip-manufacturing plants in the U.S. over the coming years, expanding its operations in Arizona. That would be on top of the $65 billion investment commitment it made during the Biden administration.

Write to Kimberley Kao at kimberley.kao@wsj.com

data.title: Bridgewater Boosts Stakes in Alibaba, Baidu in Q1
data.description:
US hedge fund Bridgewater Associates boosted its stakes in Alibaba , Baidu , and bought back into JD.com in the first quarter, according to a Wednesday filing with the US Securities and Exchange Commission.

Bridgewater's shareholding in Alibaba is now at 5,660,258 shares, equivalent to $748.5 million, the SEC said Wednesday. It rose from 255,023 shares, equivalent to $21.6 million, in the linked quarter, according to a Form 13F document issued in February.

Bridgewater's stake in Baidu also rose to 2,076,305 shares, or $191.1 million, from 196,590 shares, or $16.6 million, the filings said.

The hedge fund also purchased 2,786,833 shares in JD.com for about $114.6 million.

The US investor exited from JD.com three years ago, according to a Yicai Global report filed Thursday.

data.title: Shenzhen Composite Index Ends 0.2% Higher at 1986.50
Shanghai Composite Index Ends 0.4% Lower at 3367.46
Jari Koister nominated to the Board of White Pearl Technology Group
data.description:
Stockholm, Sweden – May 16, 2025. White Pearl Technology Group AB (Nasdaq First North Growth Market Stockholm: WPTG; OTCQX: WPTGF) today announced that the nomination committee has nominated Mr. Jari Koister to be elected to the Board of Directors at the May 20 Annual General Meeting.

As an executive technology leader, Mr. Jari Koister brings a strong track record of transforming and scaling organizations while uncovering new market opportunities in data, AI, machine learning, and cloud services. He has held senior leadership roles at FICO, Oracle, Twitter, Salesforce, and CommerceOne, where he combined deep technical expertise with strategic vision to drive innovation, accelerate growth, and deliver measurable business impact.

Solid background in entrepreneurship and technology

In addition to his professional expertise, Mr. Koister holds a Ph.D. in Technology and MS in Math and Computer Science. Mr. Koister has also a position of Professor of Data Science at the University of California, Berkeley.

“With a solid background in entrepreneurship and technology, Jari will bring invaluable expertise and experience to the Board of Directors in WPTG, further facilitating our path to profitable growth both organically and through acquisitions”, commented Mr. Ebrahim Laher, founder and main shareholder at WPTG. “Jari’ has a near perfect background to support our strategy which – as previously communicated – has set a target of 827 MSEK in global revenue with an EBITDA margin of >17% by the year 2028.”

As earlier announced, Mr. Sven Otto Littorin is proposed for re-election to the board and also proposed to be re-elected as chairman.

Current board members Arne Nabseth and Marco Marangoni is nominated för re-election while Mrs Anna Weiner Jiffer has declined re-election.

In the group proposed, Mr. Marco Marangoni is the CEO of WPTG, while the other proposed board members are independent in relation to the company, the management as well the major shareholders.

The nomination committee in WPTG is Mr. Ebrahim Laher and Mr. Bo Lindén, jointly representing a majority of shares in the company.

-end-

data.title: KOSPI Up for 5th Week
data.description:
The benchmark KOSPI rose 0.2% to close at 2,623 points on Friday and advanced 1.9% for the week, marking its fifth straight weekly gain, as weaker-than-expected US data suggested the Federal Reserve may cut rates twice this year, prompting a similar policy outlook in South Korea.

Investors also assessed data showing a third straight monthly drop in import prices, further reinforcing bets of easing by the Bank of Korea, while awaiting developments in ongoing US-Korea tariff talks.

In corporate news, chip giant SK Hynix rallied 2.1% after reporting US sales accounted for 72% of its Q1 revenue, up from 50% a year earlier, fueled by strong demand for AI-related chips.

Investors also noted SK Hynix’s fully booked HBM production for the year, reinforcing optimism in the chip sector, the country’s key export industry.

Notable gains extended to defense, shipbuilding, and auto stocks, such as Hanwha Aerospace (2.1%), Hanwha Ocean (0.8%), and Kia Corp (0.9%).

data.title: PointsBet Holdings Pays AU$500,800 Fine in Spam Penalty, Enters Court Enforceable Undertaking
data.description:
PointsBet Holdings paid a AU$500,800 spam penalty and is subject to enforceable undertakings after breaching Australia's e-marketing and gambling self-exclusion laws, according to a Friday release from the Australian Communications and Media Authority.

Investigations by ACMA alleged that the company sent more than 800 messages that breached Australia's spam laws.

It is also alleged that the firm delayed closing accounts of customers who had registered and were sending marketing messages to self-excluded persons. It sent seven marketing emails without recipient consent and 90 commercial texts that did not have sender contact information.

The authority found that no excluded customers were able to place bets with PointsBet during the period investigated, and it accepted comprehensive court-enforceable undertakings from PointsBet committing it to reviews into its compliance with spam and the National self-exclusion register laws, actioning any recommended improvements, and providing regular training for all relevant staff.

A failure to comply with an enforceable undertaking can lead to court-ordered financial penalties.

data.title: PRESSR: WeRide launches fully driverless Robotaxi Trial Operations in Abu Dhabi, broadens service coverage
data.description:
ABU DHABI, UAE /PRNewswire/ — WeRide , a global leader in autonomous driving technology, announced the launch of fully driverless Robotaxi trial operations in Abu Dhabi – the first deployment of its kind in the Middle East. Starting this quarter, a fleet of WeRide's Robotaxis will be piloted on public roads without a safety driver, marking a major milestone for smart mobility in Abu Dhabi.

Under this new phase, WeRide will also be extending its Robotaxi service coverage to include Al Maryah Island, the city's financial hub housing the Abu Dhabi Global Market; and Al Reem Island, a vibrant residential district. These new zones build on WeRide's existing Robotaxi network, which has been safely serving Yas Island, Saadiyat Island, and routes connecting to Zayed International Airport since 2021.

"WeRide makes history as the first company to pilot fully driverless Robotaxis in the Middle East region, showcasing our autonomous leadership in one of the world's most dynamic urban environments," said Jennifer Li, CFO and Head of International at WeRide. "In parallel, we're expanding commercial service to high-demand areas like Al Maryah and Al Reem Islands – bringing smart mobility solutions to more users in the Middle East. Together, these demonstrate our technological maturity while moving us closer to mass commercialization of Robotaxis."

WeRide is accelerating its global expansion, with the UAE as a key market. In July 2023, the company received the UAE's first and only national license for self-driving vehicles, authorizing testing and operation of autonomous vehicles on public roads nationwide. In December 2024, WeRide and Uber launched their ride-hailing partnership in Abu Dhabi, marking the largest commercial Robotaxi service outside the US and China.

Commercial driverless rides and additional service areas will be introduced from summer 2025, subject to regulatory approval.

About WeRide

WeRide is a global leader and a first mover in the autonomous driving industry, as well as the first publicly traded Robotaxi company. The company operates in over 30 cities across 10 countries, holding driverless permits in China, the UAE, Singapore, France and the US. Empowered by the smart, versatile, cost-effective, and highly adaptable WeRide One platform, WeRide provides autonomous driving products and services from L2 to L4, addressing transportation needs in the mobility, logistics, and sanitation industries. WeRide was named in Fortune Magazine's 2024 "The Future 50" list.

data.title: Aemulus Unit Bags Orders Worth About MYR5 Million
data.description:
Aemulus unit Aemulus Corp. bagged new orders worth $1.1 million, or about 4.7 million ringgit, on Thursday, according to a same-day Malaysian bourse filing.

The orders were awarded by a new Malaysia-based customer from the automotive industry for test systems used in automotive devices.

The secured orders are slated to be fulfilled within the financial years, 2025 and 2026, it said.

data.title: Swiss Annual Industrial Production Climbs 8.5% in Q1
data.description: Switzerland's industrial production jumped 8.5% year over year in the first quarter, following a revised 2.1% growth in the prior three-month period, the country's Federal Statistical Office said Friday.

data.title: Decent Cybersecurity to Showcase Post-Quantum Solutions at DSEI Japan 2025
data.description:
PIESTANY, SLOVAKIA / ACCESS Newswire / May 16, 2025 / Decent Cybersecurity announces its participation at DSEI Japan 2025 as the only Slovak cybersecurity company exhibiting at this premier defense event in Makuhari Messe, Chiba (May 21-23, 2025).

The company will showcase its advanced post-quantum security solutions for aerospace, defense, and space sectors, including SpaceShield STM, DroneCrypt UTM, and QuantumProof Protocol.

"The Indo-Pacific region represents a critical frontier for post-quantum cybersecurity innovation as quantum computing advances threaten traditional encryption," said Matej Michalko, Founder, Chairman and CEO of Decent Cybersecurity. "Our participation demonstrates our commitment to offering Japanese defense and space agencies access to European-certified, quantum-resistant security solutions that protect critical infrastructure against emerging threats."

As a holder of NATO, EU, and national security clearances at the "Secret" level, Decent Cybersecurity brings unique post-quantum security credentials to the Japanese defense market, where cybersecurity partnerships are increasingly prioritized amid regional security challenges.

Visit stand H7-158 at DSEI Japan 2025.

About Decent Cybersecurity:

Decent Cybersecurity is a forerunner in cybersecurity, utilizing AI, post-quantum cryptography and quantum-resistant blockchain for enhanced data protection. The company serves space and satellite communications, aerospace and defense, drone and unmanned traffic management, and government sectors. ISO 9001 and ISO 27001 certified by TÜV SÜD, Decent Cybersecurity holds national, EU, and NATO security clearances at the Secret level. With offices across Europe (Slovakia, Czech Republic, Poland, Ireland, UK), the company is registered with the European Space Agency and European Commission, providing cutting-edge solutions that safeguard critical information and infrastructure in an increasingly complex threat landscape.

Contact:

Media Relations

media@decentcybersecurity.eu

SOURCE: Decent Cybersecurity s.r.o.

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: Inspiration Energy Corp. Engages Plutus Invest & Consulting GmbH for European Marketing and Investor Awareness Services
data.description:
Vancouver, British Columbia--(Newsfile Corp. - May 16, 2025) - Inspiration Energy Corp. (WKN: A40GPX) (OTC Pink: ISPNF) ("Inspiration" or the "Company") is pleased to announce that it has engaged Plutus Invest & Consulting GmbH ("Plutus") to provide certain marketing and investor awareness services in accordance with the policies of the Canadian Securities Exchange ("CSE") and applicable legislation.

Under the terms of the agreement, effective May 15, 2025, Plutus - through its representative Marco Messina - will deliver strategic advertising, public relations, and investor communications services to the Company over a 12-month term ending May 14, 2026. These services will be focused on enhancing Inspiration's visibility and engagement within European markets.

Plutus has a business address at Buchtstr. 13, Bremen, 28195, Germany, and can be contacted by email at contact@plutuinves.de or by telephone at +49-421-1754-0174.

Inspiration has agreed to pay Plutus a fee of up to €250,000, based on the scope of services provided during the term. The agreement includes provisions for fee adjustments and early termination, if applicable.

Plutus (including its directors and officers) is an arm's-length party to the Company. To the best of the Company's knowledge, neither Plutus nor any related parties hold or have the right to acquire securities of Inspiration Energy Corp. The Company will not issue any securities to Plutus as compensation for its services.

"We are excited to partner with Plutus Invest & Consulting GmbH to expand Inspiration's presence in the European investment community," said Charles Desjardins, President of Inspiration Energy Corp. "Their expertise in strategic communications and investor outreach will support our efforts to raise awareness, strengthen our brand, and connect with a broader base of investors as we advance our growth plans - particularly as we move forward with our flagship gold-copper project in Saskatchewan, which directly borders and is on trend with Ramp Metals Corp.'s recent discovery at its Rottenstone Project."

The Company also announces that pursuant to its stock option plan, the Company has granted incentive stock options to certain directors, officers, consultants and employees to purchase a total of 1,500,000 common shares in the capital stock of the Company, exercisable for a period of five years at a price of $0.21 per common share.

About Inspiration Energy Corp.

Inspiration Energy Corp. is engaged in the business of mineral exploration and the acquisition of mineral property assets in Canada. Its objective is to locate and develop properties of merit and to conduct exploration on the Company's properties. For more information, please refer to the Company's information available on SEDAR+ (www.sedarplus.ca).

On Behalf of the Board of Directors

Charles Desjardins

CEO, President and Director

Phone: 604-808-3156

data.title: REG-Reporting of transactions made by members of the Board of Directors or Executive Management or their Closely Associated Persons
data.description:
Pursuant to the Market Abuse Regulation, article 19, Svitzer Group A/S, CVR-no. 44 79 14 47, (“Svitzer”) hereby notifies receipt of information of the following transactions made by members of the Board of Directors or Executive Management in Svitzer or their closely associated persons in Svitzer’s shares admitted to trading and official listing on Nasdaq Copenhagen A/S in connection with APMH Invest A/S’ all-cash voluntary recommended purchase offer to acquire all issued shares of Svitzer, excluding any Svitzer treasury shares and shares held by APMH Invest A/S, on the terms set out in the offer document published by APMH Invest A/S on 2 April 2025.

About Svitzer

Svitzer is a leading, global towage and marine services provider. The core business is to assist large seaborne vessels in manoeuvring in and out of ports and terminals to berth and unberth. With around 450 vessels, Svitzer’s services play a crucial role as part of critical port infrastructure. Svitzer was founded in 1833 and serves approximately 2,000 customers in more than 140 ports and 40 terminals across 37 countries. Read more on www.svitzer.com.

data.title: Australian Shares Rise Amidst Strong US Economic Data; Helia Says CBA, Affiliates Become Substantial Holder
data.description:
Australian Shares rose on Friday's close, tracking Wall Street gains, as April US producer price data increased the possibility of further interest rate cuts this year.

The S&P/ASX 200 Index rose 0.56% or 46.2 points to close at 8,343.7.

US producer prices fell by 0.5% in April, the biggest drop in five years, suggesting that businesses are taking on more of the cost from higher tariffs instead of raising prices for consumers, Bloomberg reported.

Wells Fargo Investment Institute expects earnings growth to fuel further stock-market gains this year but warned that returns may slow from current highs.

"Our constructive equity outlook is not an all clear for the riskiest areas of the markets," the team said.

On the domestic front, while the 90-day trade truce between the US and China points to progress, the Trump administration's tariff policy will continue to dent the global outlook, reinforcing the need for central banks to cut rates, ANZ Research said.

CommBank's economic analysts expect the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA) to cut the cash rate by 25 basis points to 3.85% at its meeting on May 2 and leave the door open for another easing in July, according to a report.

Non-property sector steel demand in China is forecast to grow almost 2% this year, supporting iron ore demand.

In company news, Helia Group received notice that Commonwealth Bank of Australia and its affiliates became substantial holders of the lender's mortgage insurance provider.

Dexus received a notice from the board of Australia Pacific Airports alleging that it used a confidentiality deed poll and disclosed confidential information in the Melbourne and Launceston airports sale process in breach of the requirements under the shareholders' deed.

Lastly, Appen expects revenue of $235 million to $260 million and sees positive underlying earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization for the current year. Shares of the company rose 19% at market close.

data.title: Technodex Names CFO
data.description:
Technodex appointed Tam Yun Kiam as the chief financial officer of the company, effective Thursday, May 15, according to a same-day Malaysian bourse filing.

He was appointed as the company's financial controller back in July 2019.

data.title: Harbin Pharmaceutical to Fully Acquire Sanjing Qianhe for 23 Million Yuan; Shares Up 4%
data.description:
Harbin Pharmaceutical Group plans to fully acquire Harbin Pharmaceutical Group Sanjing Qianhe Pharmaceutical for 23.4 million yuan, according to a Friday disclosure on the Shanghai bourse.

The Chinese pharmaceutical company, through unit Harbin Pharmaceutical Group Sanjing Pharmaceutical, will acquire the remaining 42.82% that it does not yet hold in Sanjing Qianhe.

After the acquisition, Sanjing Pharma's stake in Sanjing Qianhe will rise from 57.18% to 100%.

The company's shares jumped 4% in recent trade.
